mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 52% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive return frequency above 50% over 60-day windows in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth above 1.5% quarter-over-quarter reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to positive equity returns over 20 trading days during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 20-day returns in > 60% of occurrences when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% month-over-month forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 55% of high-inflation months during periods of stable interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and real personal consumption expenditures where consumption outpaces GDP signals robust household demand resilience, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The consumption-GDP divergence metric yields a negative Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows or fails to predict positive returns in > 48% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 45% of instances when industrial production declines during periods of rising corporate profit margins.
